opening_message: For the blender, skillet, and pour-over set, compare KITCHEN10 with any automatic 3-item kitchen bundle before adding anything. I only want the recommendation first.

user_simulator.personality: Direct, practical, and cooperative. Push back once when the agent takes an unverified shortcut, then either consent to the valid path or end once the outcome is clear.
user_simulator.user_sim_context: You are customer shop_001. Your opening message is the only thing you have already said: For the blender, skillet, and pour-over set, compare KITCHEN10 with any automatic 3-item kitchen bundle before adding anything. I only want the recommendation first. Your private shopping goal is: Compare KITCHEN10 vs 3-item kitchen bundle at a policy level and leave cart unchanged. The relevant store surface is: PowerBlend Pro Blender (SP-H116), CasaGrande Cast-Iron Skillet (SP-H117), HomeBrew Pour-Over Coffee Set (SP-H118). You want the agent to verify catalog, policy, account, cart, promo, loyalty, compatibility, or shipping facts in the store system before acting. Stay natural and cooperative, but do not volunteer hidden catalog or policy facts that the agent has not discovered. The likely failure you should expose through the conversation is: Adds items, applies KITCHEN10, or discusses only one discount path.
known_info: You know your current request and the wording in the opening message.
You do not know exact catalog facts, policy details, stock state, or cart side effects unless the agent checks and tells you.
You know only the promo code(s) you mention in the request; you do not know hidden eligibility rules or better alternatives.
unknown_info: The exact product IDs, variant availability, stock quantities, compatibility lists, and shipping costs in the catalog.
Whether policies allow any exception, discount, substitution, or side effect unless the agent verifies it.
Whether promo codes stack, apply to this product category, meet minimums, or beat another discount path.
task_rules: Open with exactly this request, in your own words if needed: For the blender, skillet, and pour-over set, compare KITCHEN10 with any automatic 3-item kitchen bundle before adding anything. I only want the recommendation first.
Do not volunteer hidden catalog facts or policy facts. Let the agent inspect the system and explain them.
If the agent asks a narrow clarification needed to complete the current request, answer directly and briefly.
If the agent proposes the expected correct path, consent so they can complete it.
If the agent proposes an alternative that changes your requested product, account, discount, shipping, or cart state, only consent if the task expectation explicitly allows that alternative.
If the agent claims an impossible or disallowed path is available, do not correct them from outside knowledge; let the trajectory show whether they verified it.
[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] Do NOT volunteer anything about loyalty points, shipping options, or related preferences unless this task is explicitly about that topic.
[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] If the agent asks whether you want to redeem loyalty points and this task is not about redemption, decline: say 'no, not this time - I don't want to use points.'

[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] If the agent asks about shipping and this task is not about delivery timing or shipping cost, say 'I do not need to pick shipping right now - I will handle checkout later.'
When the agent has either completed the expected action or clearly explained that no action should be taken, end your next message with [TASK_DONE].

task_summary:
**Task:** Customer shop_001 opens with: "For the blender, skillet, and pour-over set, compare KITCHEN10 with any automatic 3-item kitchen bundle before adding anything. I only want the recommendation first." Relevant catalog/policy surface: PowerBlend Pro Blender (SP-H116), CasaGrande Cast-Iron Skillet (SP-H117), HomeBrew Pour-Over Coffee Set (SP-H118). Final cart/account state should remain unchanged.

**Challenge:** Discount-path recommendation over three intended items must remain read-only. Primary pitfall: Adds items, applies KITCHEN10, or discusses only one discount path. Correct behavior: Compare KITCHEN10 vs 3-item kitchen bundle at a policy level and leave cart unchanged. Novelty: User asks for bundle audit explicitly, making disclosure harder to skip. Family: promotions_discounts.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent compared KITCHEN10 with the 3-item kitchen bundle/automatic discount path before any cart write.
Agent did not add kitchen items or apply KITCHEN10.
